definition: Structured consultative process that discovers buyer needs holistically before pitching solutions.
rationale: Centroid term too generic; framework is about discovery-first consultative selling, not generic 'sales framework.'

splits.definition: Minimize cash gap between customer acquisition and payment collection to enable leverage.
Non-traditional payment/financing structures (seller financing, installments, billing rhythm) to improve cash position.
Shifting payment terms with suppliers to free cash without product changes.

definition: Strategic management of cash inflows/outflows through payment timing, terms, and financing.
rationale: Cluster bundles three distinct mechanisms: customer payment timing, alternative financing structures, and supplier terms—too heterogeneous, requires split.

aliases: Nail It Then Scale It Framework
definition: Perfect a single unit's unit economics and operations before replicating to new locations.
rationale: Centroid name overly branded; operative principle is testing single-unit viability before multiunit expansion.

Cluster mixes philosophical/meta-personal concepts (situational ethics, life struggle) with operational risk (decision limbo); fails B3 (resolvable context) and B4 (load-bearing clarity); too abstract for actionable knowledge graph.

definition: Partners aligned by absorbing shared information reduce divergence in thinking and decision-making.
rationale: Core claim is coherent (shared knowledge ↔ relationship capacity); 'Ignorance as Evil' is vague philosophy without mechanism—kick it; merge surviving pair into relationship/partnership concept.

splits.definition: Use platform-native targeting to narrow cold audiences to highest-probability buyer segments.
Close rates spike when messaging targets only those with actual buying power and decision authority.

definition: Narrow audience targeting improves conversion by focusing spend on qualified, high-probability segments.
rationale: Market Distribution (1.2M followers) is metric without context/mechanism—kick; split platform-targeting tactics from buyer-qualification principle.

splits.definition: Teach AI systems with explicit, granular, observable steps and iterative feedback—like onboarding a junior employee.
Half-built AI systems fail vs. optimized human workflows because they lack equivalent training time and integration depth.

definition: Effective AI deployment requires same training rigor and system integration as human workforce scaling.
rationale: Cluster conflates training methodology with maturity/integration gotchas; 'Five AI Workers' is orphaned feature-list—kick it; split training principle from implementation risk.

definition: Mass-market scaling demands capital and operational excellence; high-end markets are bootstrappable.
rationale: Coherent axis (market position ↔ capital need); all members load-bearing and conditional; centroid is strong.

definition: In deal-dependent businesses, pipeline availability is the constraint, not execution skill; gatekeepers control access.
rationale: Centroid clear but imprecise; operative insight is that pipeline is primary constraint; 'Building Under Uncertainty' lacks mechanism; 'Lead Gen to Coaching' is specific tactical pivot without general principle.

definition: E-commerce is capital-intensive with fast growth until supply chain or distribution constraints bind.
rationale: Coherent model shape; all members directly address capital and operational tradeoffs; high min_intra_sim.

definition: Extract top performer behaviors into teachable, codified systems; sales is exceptionally replicable skill.
rationale: Strong centroid; all 10 members address same mechanism (extract and teach specific behaviors); high practical applicability.

definition: Start at premium/high-impact tier (automation, positioning, channels); work downward rather than bootstrap-up.
rationale: Cluster principle is clear (top-down > bottom-up); Tesla example anchors well; rename to reflect broader principle than 'Automation' alone.

Cluster contains contradictory claims: members #2 and #3 directly contradict on whether shorts funnel to longs; fails B1 (self-identifying type); too specific/tactical (platform feature) without general principle; lacks mechanism.

definition: Clients resist public testimonials due to NDA, tax liability, and cancel-culture risk; implementation barrier, not strategic edge.
rationale: Centroid weak; operative insight is market friction, not opportunity—rename to reflect constraint; valuable for practitioners planning proof strategies.

definition: SaaS/service unit economics vary by price tier; lower price (< $600/mo) trades higher churn for volume; optimize CAC accordingly.
rationale: Coherent quantified axis (price band ↔ LTV/CAC/churn); all members load-bearing with numbers anchored; high practical diagnostic value.
